explicit_text: My parents lived in Laos before we moved to Thailand and eventually the United States. They chose not to come to the United States initially because they were worried something might happen to us. Laos was where my father's basket-making skills were deeply rooted, as he learned to make bamboo baskets since he was a kid there.
implicit_text: My parents lived in a Southeast Asian country before we moved to Thailand and eventually the United States. They chose not to come to the United States initially because they were worried something might happen to us. That country was where my father's basket-making skills were deeply rooted, as he learned to make bamboo baskets since he was a kid there.
cues: father learned basket making as a kid
parents hesitant to move to the United States

explicit_text: My family and I came to the United States in 2004 after living in Thailand. Although some Hmong people had already moved to the United States, my parents were cautious at first. Now, living here, we still use the traditional bamboo baskets to show others their importance and to teach the new generation about our culture.
implicit_text: My family and I came to North America in 2004 after living in Thailand. Although some Hmong people had already moved to this country, my parents were cautious at first. Now, living here, we still use the traditional bamboo baskets to show others their importance and to teach the new generation about our culture.
cues: moved to the United States in 2004

explicit_text: I was born in Thailand where my family lived before coming to the United States. While in Thailand, my mother taught me how to sew paj ntaub so I could help earn money for our family. Many Hmong people, including some from my community, lived in Thailand during that time before resettling elsewhere.
implicit_text: I was born in the country just south of the one where my parents first lived, before coming to the United States. While there, my mother taught me how to sew paj ntaub so I could help earn money for our family. Many Hmong people, including some from my community, lived in that country during that time before resettling elsewhere.

explicit_text: Paj ntaub is a traditional Hmong embroidery that my mother taught me to sew while we were in Thailand. Sewing paj ntaub was a way for the women in our family to earn money, although people only paid about twenty dollars for it. I learned this skill to help support my parents financially, and it remains an important part of our family’s livelihood.
implicit_text: This traditional Hmong embroidery was taught to me by my mother while we were living in Thailand. Sewing it was a way for the women in our family to earn money, although people only paid about twenty dollars for it. I learned this skill to help support my parents financially, and it remains an important part of our family’s livelihood.

explicit_text: During the Hmong New Year, some girls dance while carrying bamboo baskets, which is a tradition in our culture. This event is a special time when we celebrate and showcase important cultural items like the baskets my father makes. It helps keep our customs alive and connects the younger generation to our heritage.
implicit_text: During the traditional New Year celebration of our people, some girls dance while carrying bamboo baskets, which is a tradition in our culture. This event is a special time when we celebrate and showcase important cultural items like the baskets my father makes. It helps keep our customs alive and connects the younger generation to our heritage.
